channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: One year later: Mahmoud Khalil remains in limbo but ready to fight
item.description: The case of Khalil, who was detained last March, sits at the vanguard of a battle of immigrants' due process and civil rights, and the Trump administration's mass detention and deportation policies.
item.content:encoded:
The case of Khalil, who was detained last March, sits at the vanguard of a battle of immigrants' due process and civil rights, and the Trump administration's mass detention and deportation policies.

(Image credit: Stephanie Keith)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Is the Iran war another Iraq? This expert sees parallels
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Richard Haass, president emeritus of the Council on Foreign Relations, about the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Richard Haass, president emeritus of the Council on Foreign Relations, about the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zamora

item.title: Move over, goat yoga — in Portland, Ore., you can do snake yoga
item.description: You've heard of puppy yoga, and goat yoga, and maybe even reindeer yoga... but what about yoga with a bunch of pythons and one baby Colombian common boa named Mango?
item.content:encoded: You've heard of puppy yoga, and goat yoga, and maybe even reindeer yoga... but what about yoga with a bunch of pythons and one baby Colombian common boa named Mango?
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: Tennessee could place some foster youth into jail-like facilities under new bill
item.description: There aren't enough foster homes to place children in Tennessee. Some fear that a new proposal to put foster kids with serious behavioral issues into jail-like facilities will make things worse.
item.content:encoded: There aren't enough foster homes to place children in Tennessee. Some fear that a new proposal to put foster kids with serious behavioral issues into jail-like facilities will make things worse.
item.dc:creator: Paige Pfleger

item.title: As U.S. and Israel strike Iran, China's foreign policy experts question Washington
item.description: The U.S. and Israeli joint attacks on Iran have prompted alarm and intense discussion among China's foreign policy elite as they prepare for a U.S. presidential visit.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Israeli joint attacks on Iran have prompted alarm and intense discussion among China's foreign policy elite as they prepare for a U.S. presidential visit.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Water restrictions hit home in California
item.description: In California's greatest farming region, there's a water crisis from overpumping groundwater. The state passed a law in 2014 to restrict overdrawing the aquifers, and the limits are going into effect.
item.content:encoded: In California's greatest farming region, there's a water crisis from overpumping groundwater. The state passed a law in 2014 to restrict overdrawing the aquifers, and the limits are going into effect.
item.dc:creator: Dan Charles

item.title: Punch the macaque, an internet sensation, is making strides in monkey society
item.description: Punch, a Japanese macaque, stole the hearts of millions after he was abandoned by his mom and rejected by some of his peers. Now, things are looking up for him.
item.content:encoded: Punch, a Japanese macaque, stole the hearts of millions after he was abandoned by his mom and rejected by some of his peers. Now, things are looking up for him.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Iraqi Kurds rush to quash reports of Kurds leading uprising in Iran
item.description: Kurdistan's deputy prime minister tells NPR that Kurdish forces will not enter Iran or join the war, insisting "this is not our war" despite pressure from Washington and regional tensions.
item.content:encoded: Kurdistan's deputy prime minister tells NPR that Kurdish forces will not enter Iran or join the war, insisting "this is not our war" despite pressure from Washington and regional tensions.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Why Ukraine is offering to help U.S. in drone warfare with Iran
item.description: Ukraine was forced to become a world leader in drone warfare due to Russia's invasion. The lessons Ukraine learned will now be used to help the U.S. and its allies facing a drone onslaught from Iran.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine was forced to become a world leader in drone warfare due to Russia's invasion. The lessons Ukraine learned will now be used to help the U.S. and its allies facing a drone onslaught from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Trump says U.S. is 'achieving major strides' in Iran but doesn't cite endpoint
item.description: President Trump held his first news conference since the beginning of the U.S.-Israel-led Iran war on Monday as oil and gas prices soared, throwing the global economy into turmoil.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump held his first news conference since the beginning of the U.S.-Israel-led Iran war on Monday as oil and gas prices soared, throwing the global economy into turmoil.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller
